claim: Compose different LLM models for different workflow phases: use strongest reasoning model for planning, speed/cost-optimized model for UI generation, strongest for integrations
actionable: Route planning tasks to Opus, UI generation to Gemini 3.5 Flash, integration/backend to Opus; separate sessions via handoff documents keep context windows clean and models focused
source_title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends

claim: Gemini 3.5 Flash generates significantly more beautiful and human-like UIs than Opus, but Opus is stronger at reasoning about content accuracy and preventing hallucination
actionable: For full-stack apps, delegate UI/design tasks to Gemini Flash; delegate content, integrations, and logic to Opus; compose results to maximize quality across dimensions
source_title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends

claim: Each step in multi-model workflows should be a separate coding agent session with handoff documents, not a single continuous context, to keep context windows clean and enable easy provider swapping
actionable: Design workflows with discrete nodes (exploration, planning, design, integration, validation); pass outputs as markdown files between sessions; enables mixing models without context window bloat
source_title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends

claim: Opus in planning should explicitly avoid describing UI structure/layout decisions; let Gemini design those, as Opus over-specifying UI reduces quality of Gemini's designs
actionable: In planning phase, instruct Opus to describe content, integrations, and data flow only; do not include UI layout details; these should emerge from Gemini's design phase
source_title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends

claim: Workflow should include exploration, planning, design, integration, validation, and smoke testing (agent browser automation) steps to one-shot MVPs and simple full-stack apps
actionable: Use 6-7 step workflow: explore repo/spec, plan content/integrations, design UI, implement backend, validate tests, smoke test with browser automation, then deploy
source_title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends

claim: Large LLMs get overwhelmed when asked to handle exploration, planning, design, and integration in a single session; splitting into focused sessions improves quality and reliability
actionable: Never ask a single agent session to handle 4+ distinct tasks; split into focused 1-2 task sessions; reduces token waste, improves output quality, enables provider-specific optimization
source_title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
